Ad Text:
With more than 218,000 members we’ve been a part of the Tasmanian community for over 100 years – we're Tasmania’s shoulder to lean on and voice when it matters. Our values guide us in all that we do – Engage with Heart, Unleash Potential, Walk the Talk and Together we Thrive. With our local knowledge and as the state’s number one brand, we’re here to support our members. 

As RACT continues to transform and create more value for Tasmanians, we have a unique opportunity for someone to join our Transformation team as our Manager Portfolio Insights.

RACT’s Change Agenda is growing in scale, complexity, and expectation. The Manager Portfolio Insights is a newly created role with a unique opportunity to build, from the ground up, the analytical intelligence, visualisation capability, and decision-support infrastructure that will underpin how the ELT and GCEO prioritise, resource, and govern change across the organisation.

Some of your accountabilities will include:

Design and maintain RACT’s first integrated portfolio view of all active and pipeline initiatives, including status, budget, resourcing, dependencies, and strategic alignment.

Build a capacity and resource model integrating PMO and Technology delivery constraints, giving leadership a consolidated view of what the organisation can realistically absorb and when.

Develop an internal rate card for PMO resourcing and establish a portfolio benefits register in collaboration with Finance and project sponsors, tracking realisation against approved business cases.

Design high-quality portfolio visualisations that translate complex, multi-dimensional data into clear narratives for the GCEO, ELT, and Board, making prioritisation trade-offs immediately legible to senior decision-makers.

Develop and maintain interactive dashboards providing real-time visibility of portfolio health, financial performance, delivery risk, and capacity utilisation.

Prepare concise, decision-ready briefing materials for governance forums and ELT prioritisation discussions.

Build the analytical evidence base for portfolio prioritisation, modelling trade-offs across strategic alignment, delivery capacity, risk, cost, and benefit to support more rigorous ELT decision-making.

Contribute to continuous improvement of PMO frameworks, standards, and tools; coach stakeholders in interpreting portfolio data and maintaining planning accuracy.

A copy of the full position description can be provided by emailing peopleandculture@ract.com.au

Successful candidates will have:

5 or more years’ experience in portfolio management, PMO, or strategic analytics, with demonstrated ability to build reporting, insight, and decision‑support capabilities.

Advanced analytical and data visualisation capability, including capacity modelling, scenario planning, and benefits tracking, using Power BI, Tableau, or equivalent tools.

Proven experience producing clear, decision‑grade materials for executive leadership and board audiences.

Strong understanding of delivery environments, including experience working with technology functions and agile or hybrid lifecycles.

High proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint, Jira, and MS Project (or equivalent enterprise tools).

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to synthesize complex information into concise, compelling portfolio narratives.

Relevant qualifications or certification in business, data analytics, or project management, with experience in regulated, financial, or member‑based organisations viewed favourably.

At RACT, we’re not just a workplace – we’re a community. When you join our team, you become part of something bigger. Your ideas and the work you do will help shape our community and environment.

Here’s why you’ll love being part of our team:

Staff discounts: Enjoy significant discounts on RACT home and car insurance, and free Ultimate Roadside Assistance.

Wellbeing leave: Three days of Wellbeing Leave available per year that can be used however you wish.

Superannuation contribution matching: For every dollar you contribute to your superannuation, we’ll match it up to 2%.

Career growth: We invest in your development because your success is our success.

Flexible work: Life isn’t one-size-fits-all, and neither is our work. Balance your life commitments with our flexible work environment.

By joining RACT you'll be a part of an inclusive culture that celebrates diversity – every individual is valued and respected. We invite people from all backgrounds to apply. 

Ready to join the RACT family? We would love to hear from you!

Reach out to our People and Culture Team at peopleandculture@ract.com.au if you have any questions about this role or working with RACT.

Please submit your application via SEEK and attach an up-to-date resume and cover letter by 9am Friday 15 May 2026.

Job Title: FP&A Reporting Manager - Transformation Focus - 12 Month FTC
Ad Text:
Our client is an established ASX-listed consumer products company with a strong portfolio of recognisable brands. Finance is undergoing a period of deliberate transformation, and this fixed term hire is central to that agenda.

About the Role

This role is a 12 Month FTC with a view to go permanent. Reporting to the Head of FP&A, the FP&A Reporting Manager will contribute to core group planning and reporting activities, while directing the majority of their focus toward transformation. In the near term that means improving the tools, models, and processes that underpin FP&A — driver-based forecasting, reporting automation, planning system enhancements, and better data visibility across the group.

Within six months, the role is expected to transition into a full-time FP&A representative on an ERP implementation — working directly with finance, IT, and the implementation partner to ensure the new system is built around how the business actually plans, forecasts, and reports.

If you're someone who finds the build more interesting than the BAU, this role is worth a conversation.

What You'll Bring

Solid group or divisional FP&A experience, ideally in consumer, FMCG, or retail
Demonstrated experience implementing FP&A tools and processes — forecasting models, reporting automation, planning system enhancements
Hands-on experience with planning and BI platforms such as TM1, Anaplan, Power BI, or equivalent is ideal
Exposure to ERP environments and how finance data flows through integrated systems — prior ERP implementation experience preferred but not essential.
A genuine process improvement mindset with the confidence to challenge how things are currently done
Clear communicator who can work effectively across finance and with IT and project stakeholders
What's on Offer

A role with genuine scope beyond the day-to-day FP&A cycle
Clear pathway onto a high-visibility ERP project within six months
A business actively investing in its finance function
Competitive remuneration package commensurate with experience
To forward your details to Duncan McNeill, please apply via the button below.

Principal Digital Forensics & eDiscovery, Ongoing opportunity based in Sydney CBD + hybrid working options available

The Department of Customer Service is looking for a Principal Digital Forensics and eDiscovery lead to join our growing cyber security team! This is an influential role providing technical advice, support and recommendations on digital investigations, forensics analysis and eDiscovery processes.

Benefits

Fantastic ongoing grade 11/12 technical leadership role driving the insider risk stream, eDiscovery capabilities, and operational excellence.
Salary range: $149,739 - $173,174+ super, commensurate with experience.
Excellent leave entitlements: 20 days annual leave, plus up to an additional 24 days flexible leave.

About you

Accomplished cyber security professional with over 7 years of experience, specialising in insider risk management, threat detection, and behavioural analytics.
Proven track record in leading large-scale digital investigations, designing insider risk detection workflows, and executing complex eDiscovery strategies.
Expert in eDiscovery, insider risk platforms, UEBA, digital forensics and data analysis across diverse operating systems and file types.
Deep understanding of legal evidence handling, chain of custody, and regulatory compliance frameworks.
Strong communicator and collaborator, skilled at bridging technical and non-technical teams to drive security initiatives and investigations.
Demonstrated ability to influence stakeholders and deliver executive-level insights.
Experienced in enterprise environments with a focus on resilience, privacy, and ethical risk management.
Hold industry-recognised certifications.
Known for analytical rigor, attention to detail, and a commitment to maintaining the integrity and accuracy of sensitive investigations.

Responsibilities include

Lead a team of analysts responsible for triaging insider risk alerts, managing referrals, and conducting digital evidence collection to support investigations and cyber incident response.
Own the technical design, operation, and continuous improvement of eDiscovery, Insider Risk detection and investigation capabilities, ensuring effective identification, analysis, and containment of insider threats, data loss, and misuse events.Design and implement scalable frameworks leveraging UEBA, DLP, SIEM, and behavioural analytics to enhance insider risk detection and response capabilities.
Direct the acquisition, preservation, and analysis of digital evidence across endpoints, servers, mobile devices, and cloud environments ensuring chain of custody, and legal admissibility of evidence where required.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Legal, HR, Compliance, and IT to ensure alignment with regulatory, legal, and ethical standards.
Develop and refine policies, procedures, and playbooks to support insider risk investigations and digital evidence handling.
Provide executive-level reporting on insider risk trends, program maturity, and key performance metrics to inform strategic decision-making.
Champion a culture of trust, transparency, and accountability while balancing security with privacy.
Evaluate and integrate emerging technologies and methodologies to continuously improve insider risk posture and investigative capabilities.
Deliver expert guidance on insider threat detection and digital forensics, ensuring team readiness across diverse technology environments.
Partner with internal and external stakeholders to resolve technical challenges, deploy advanced security capabilities, and strengthen organisational resilience.
Document and present investigative findings to legal counsel, senior leadership, and relevant stakeholders with clarity and precision.

Essential Requirements: Australian citizenship with the successful applicant having, or being able to attain and maintain, a baseline security clearance or higher as a condition of employment.

If you want to be part of this forward thinking, innovative team that thrive in challenging the status quo, we would love to hear from you!

What we need from you
To start your journey towards becoming our Principal Digital Forensics and eDiscovery, please click on the link below and attach your resume (max 4 pages) and cover letter (max 2 pages).

In your cover letter please provide an overview of your suitability to the role based on the 'Responsibilities' and 'About you' sections above.

A talent pool may be created from this recruitment process to fill future ongoing and temporary opportunities.

Salary Grade 11/12, with the base salary for this role starting at $149,739 base plus superannuation

An exciting opportunity has arisen for a Business Analyst to join the People & Governance team within Sydney Water. As a Business Analyst, you will identify, analyse, and collaborate with the business to document business processes, ServiceNow platform impacts, workflows, and functional requirements to facilitate continuous improvement and support the efficient delivery of business operations and performance in line with organisational strategies and operational plans.

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

Analyse, document and collaborate in a way that ensures positive working relationships with Process Owners, Digital Group teams and end-users, resolving competing priorities and requirements in the best interests of SWC.
Collaborate, Analyse and document end-to-end process with the intent to identify areas of potential improvement, automation, and improved user experience through Service Now.
Review and sign off documentation that clearly articulates the business problem, proposed ServiceNow-based solution, and measurable value or outcomes.
Provide timely data analysis and insights that encourage constructive conversations with Process Owners to better troubleshoot issues, understand user experience and desired measurable benefits.
Gather, analyse and translate business data to meaningful insights to support informed decision making to improve the effectiveness of processes and programs.
Support Service Now Program delivery during the requirement definition, testing, change impact, go-live and hyper care phase.
Investigate and respond to service tickets and related system business user enquiries in a timely manner.
Support the management of change requests, enhancements, and incidents across ServiceNow modules in collaboration with technical teams.

 Experience and Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in business or a related field
Bachelor’s degree in information technology (welcomed by not essential)
4+ years’ experience working as a Business Analysis in a large complex organisation.
Experience in a full end to end SaaS, HR and Financial project / program implementation (Service Now desirable) to improve the solution design and ensure business requirements are met.
Must have foundational experience in Service Management System (Service New desirable)
Demonstrated business and IT experience, including expert knowledge in business analysis including the end-to-end management of change and maintenance requests and driving continuous improvement.
Experience in the research, investigation, and the provision of practical solutions to business issues. 
High level analytical and problem-solving skills.

Recruitment Process 

To apply, please submit your CV along with a covering letter explaining why you are the right candidate for this role. 

Essential Requirements: 

You must be an Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident at the time of submitting your application to be considered for this role. 

Closing Date: Wednesday 29th April 2026.

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst to support the delivery and continuous improvement of ICT and digital technology services in a complex, stakeholder-driven environment.

Working closely with business stakeholders and Digital Technologies teams, you will help assess impacts, define requirements, and shape practical, fit-for-purpose solutions. This role suits a proactive, delivery-focused Business Analyst who is comfortable operating independently, partnering with technical teams, and contributing across the full lifecycle—from discovery through to implementation support.

What You Will Be Doing 

Work with business stakeholders to identify, analyse and document business, functional and non-functional requirements
Review and assess business and ICT impacts, translating needs into clear solution scopes in collaboration with Digital Technologies teams
Research and gather requirements from existing documentation, support requests, workshops and stakeholder interviews
Support the development and maintenance of business cases, policies, procedures and system documentation
Assist stakeholders to identify and document ICT impacts, risks, contingency plans and user workarounds
Create and update process maps, diagrams and supporting artefacts as required
Provide informed advice on system capabilities, information requirements, scope management and delivery risks
Contribute to peer reviews and support other technology resources
Document methodologies, tools and structures to support ongoing business needs
Keep across industry and technology trends to identify opportunities for improvement
What You Will Bring

You will bring a strong foundation in business analysis within ICT and digital environments, along with:

Demonstrated experience as a Business Analyst delivering outcomes in technology-enabled projects or operational environments
Strong capability in requirements elicitation, stakeholder engagement and documentation
Experience working across business and technical teams to shape pragmatic solutions
Well-developed written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear documentation and reports
The ability to work independently, manage competing priorities and deliver with minimal supervision
Experience working within PRINCE2 and/or Agile delivery frameworks
A relevant tertiary qualification, or equivalent practical experience
Bonus Points For 

Experience working on or alongside Cyber Security, infrastructure or modern workplace initiatives
Knowledge of cyber security frameworks such as ASD Essential Eight and NIST
Exposure to modern cloud, endpoint and security platforms, including:
Microsoft Azure / Entra ID
Microsoft Intune
Zscaler or similar secure access / zero-trust technologies
A continuous improvement mindset and interest in emerging technology and industry trends

About the Role

We're looking for a highly skilled and experienced Senior SAP Functional Analyst to join our dynamic IT team at our Daikin Park in Warwick Farm, NSW. In this role, you will analyse the business problems and requirements to design a fully functional solution, assisting the Enterprise Architect to evaluate and finalise the SAP solutions. This is a fantastic opportunity to make a significant impact within a leading global company.

Your key responsibilities will include:


SAP System Expertise & Support: Leading the entire lifecycle of SAP Supply Chain Management (SCM) module (including WM, MM, PP) implementation and optimisation. You'll translate business needs into effective SAP solutions to enhance the supply chain, improve inventory, and optimise production, ensuring seamless integration with other SAP systems and providing functional expertise throughout project phases.

Business Process Analysis and Optimisation: Focuses on optimising supply chain management processes by analysing current operations and recommending improvements. You'll collaborate closely with business users to gather and document their requirements, then map these needs to existing or new SAP functionalities. A key objective is to identify and implement process automation and efficiency gains to streamline operations and reduce costs.


SAP Configuration and Customisation: Involved in the configuration of SAP modules like SCM (Core WM, MM and PP) to align with business requirements and best practices. You'll also develop functional specifications for custom developments, collaborating closely with the technical team. A key responsibility is to conduct and support testing phases (unit, integration, and UAT) to ensure SAP solutions function correctly and perform optimally.

Problem Resolution & Optimisation: Act as a key resource for troubleshooting and resolving critical SAP incidents and problems. You'll perform root cause analysis, implement corrective actions, and proactively identify areas for system optimisation to enhance performance and user experience.

Project Leadership & Collaboration: Play a vital role in SAP-related projects, including upgrades, migrations, and new module implementations. You'll work seamlessly with other IT teams (e.g., Basis, ABAP developers), external consultants, and business users to deliver successful outcomes.




We're looking for someone who brings:

You're a highly experienced and technically proficient SAP professional with a deep understanding of supply chain processes and a passion for optimizing systems. You're a natural problem-solver, an excellent communicator, and thrive in a dynamic, multicultural environment.

We're looking for someone who brings:

Higher education in Computer Sciences / Any Engineering Studies, and related SAP certification is mandatory.

Minimum of 10 years of hands-on/proven experience in SAP SCM functional roles covering Materials Management (MM), Production Planning (PP), Sales and Distribution (SD), and Warehouse Management (WM).

Proven experience in full life-cycle SAP implementations, including gathering requirements, configuring systems, conducting testing, and supporting go-live.

Experience in S/4HANA System conversion is a must.

Proficient and strong understanding of supply chain processes such as procurement, inventory management, production planning, and order fulfilment.

Knowledge of SQL and PL/SQL, with experience in Manhattan Scale highly preferred.


Ability to write functional specifications for custom developments and support ABAP teams as needed.

Experience with SAP interfaces, integrations, and EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) with Manhattan Scale is beneficial.




Why Join Us?

Collaborative work environment fostering a positive team culture.

Competitive salary + Superannuation + Performance bonus.

Equipment for work will be provided, such as a work laptop and work phone.

Provided with on-the-job training for continuous career growth opportunities.

Discounted Bupa Health Fund.

Flare Benefits – Discounted gift cards, novated leases, discounted health/car/home loans.

Be part of a global number 1 company in HVAC.

Be part of a dynamic team that values innovation, collaboration, and personal growth.

Free parking on site at our Warwick Farm, Daikin Park.




We thank all applicants for their interest; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Daikin Australia is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

Job Title: Lecturer/Senior Lecturer, Psychology and Cognitive Neuroscience
Ad Text:
About the Role 
Swinburne is seeking a Lecturer or Senior Lecturer in Psychology and Cognitive Neuroscience to join our high-performing academic team. This is a unique opportunity to contribute to world-class teaching, impactful research, and innovative curriculum design within a collaborative and future-focused environment.

Depending on your experience, you will contribute at either Lecturer or Senior Lecturer level across teaching, research, and curriculum development in cognitive neuroscience. In this role, you will:

Deliver engaging undergraduate and postgraduate teaching
Design and enhance contemporary, industry-relevant curriculum
Conduct high-quality, impactful research
Supervise undergraduate, postgraduate, and higher degree research students
Contribute to academic administration and professional activities

At Senior Lecturer level, you will also:

Lead courses and coordinate teaching teams
Provide academic leadership and mentorship to colleagues and students
Foster stronger links with industry and external partners (national and international)
Create and expand opportunities for student engagement in research beyond coursework

About the Department
The Department of Psychological Sciences, within the School of Health Sciences, is recognised for excellence in both teaching and research. Our research is nationally and internationally recognised and we continue to advance innovation in psychology and cognitive neuroscience.

We work closely with the Centre for Mental Health and Brain Sciences and Swinburne’s world-leading Neuroimaging Facility—the only single-site human imaging facility in Australia and New Zealand offering MEG, MRI, and EEG.


About You  
You are a collaborative and motivated academic with expertise in cognitive neuroscience and a passion for teaching and research. For both levels of appointment, you will bring:

Proven research experience using cognitive neuroscience methodologies (e.g. MRI, MEG, EEG)
A strong (Lecturer) or established (Senior Lecturer) track record of publications
Experience teaching in a university setting
Ability to contribute across a broad range of undergraduate and postgraduate offerings
Excellent communication skills and a collaborative approach

In addition, at Senior Lecturer level you will demonstrate:

Leadership experience in higher education, including course or program coordination
Experience supervising undergraduate research projects and fostering student research engagement
A strong ability to build and maintain industry and disciplinary links
A track record of mentoring staff and contributing to a positive team culture

Qualifications
A PhD or equivalent doctoral degree in psychology or related discipline.


About Swinburne University of Technology 
Swinburne’s strategy draws upon our understanding of future challenges. We choose to build Swinburne as the prototype of a new and different university – one that is truly of Technology, of Innovation, and of Entrepreneurship. We are committed to a differentiated university proposition in education and research.

Our Ad Astra strategy is the cornerstone of Swinburne’s bold ambition to lead globally in technology-driven education and research. This strategy positions us to create transformative solutions, empower learners, and partner with industry to thrive in an ever-changing world.   


What We Offer You!

Work your way with flexible working arrangements, including hybrid options and generous parental leave to support a balanced lifestyle.
Exclusive staff discounts, including discounted Medibank health insurance, 50% off Swinburne courses, and other partner offers.
Swinburne offers 17% super and salary packaging options, including car leasing and extra super contributions.

Want to learn more? Head over to our benefits page!


To Apply 
Please submit your CV and cover letter addressing the Key Selection Criteria and your suitability for this position.  

To review the Position Description and Key Selection Criteria and to apply, please scroll down to the bottom of the page. 

If you are viewing this advert from an external site, please click ‘apply’ and you will be redirected to Swinburne’s Jobs website to access the Position Description and Key Selection Criteria at the bottom of the page.

Please Note: Appointment to this position is subject to passing a Working with Children Check.

If you are experiencing technical difficulties with your application, please contact the Talent Acquisition team at talentacquisition@swin.edu.au 


Applications Close: Sunday 17th of May, at 11.00pm

Swinburne offers flexible working options contained in our leave and parenting/carer policies to support work-life balance.


Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
Swinburne has become a world-class university, driving social and economic impacts through science, technology, and innovation. As a dual-sector university, our vision is for people and technology working together to build a better world.

Central to our vision is our commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion. We pride ourselves on being an equal opportunity employer focused on attracting, retaining, and developing great talent. We work to remove barriers related to gender identity, culture, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability, and age.

We strongly encourage applicants from diverse Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. Our Moondani Toombadool Centre leads our Indigenous education and culture at Swinburne, guided by community wisdom and leadership.

We support applicants with disabilities. Adjustments can be requested at any time during the recruitment process. For Reasonable Adjustment requests, including accessible formats for the PD, application form or any other document, please contact DCR@swin.edu.au or call +61 3 9214 3550.

Please note the above number and DCR email address are for disability or reasonable adjustments queries only. General enquiries about the role can be sent to the Talent Acquisition team at talentacquisition@swin.edu.au (general enquiries will not be answered by phone).

Category:
Management (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Permanent Opportunity
Hybrid Work Environment
Lead a delivery practice supporting enterprise integration solutions for national critical systems


About the Role


As a Practice Manager supporting the Integration Services team at the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA), you will lead, coach and develop team members while working closely with project managers, technical professionals, architecture and security teams to achieve agreed delivery outcomes.


You will be responsible for establishing and maintaining standard work practices and integration design standards, including automation, to improve the quality, maturity and consistency of services, technologies and supporting processes.


Key Responsibilities


Develop, lead and mature the Integration Services delivery practice by providing people leadership, building capability, setting direction and priorities, managing demand forecasts with supply and contributing to resourcing strategies that enable high quality delivery outcomes.
Ensure operational accountability for the delivery practice by overseeing performance, compliance and adherence to agreed processes and controls.
Provide technical delivery guidance and assurance across integration initiatives, ensuring solutions meet agreed standards for quality, consistency, reliability, and operational readiness.
Partner closely with project managers, architecture, security, operations, and vendors to guide solution design, align expectations, support accurate estimation and forecasting, resolve delivery and technical risks, and achieve sustainable outcomes.
Influence and promote the adoption of common integration patterns, platforms, and automation approaches to improve reuse, efficiency, and long term maintainability.
Contribute to the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of integration standards, policies, systems, and delivery practices, ensuring alignment with enterprise architecture, industry standards, and organisational requirements.
Ensure integration solutions are delivered and transitioned to meet operational needs, supportability, sustainability, enabling effective transition into business as usual operations.
Manage stakeholder and vendor relationships, ensuring procurement and delivery engagements are effective, expectations are aligned, and issues or blockers impacting delivery outcomes are addressed in a timely manner.
Proactively identify, assess, and respond to delivery, technical, and operational risks and opportunities, maintaining delivery momentum while protecting service quality and stability.
Provide clear assurance, insight, and reporting on integration delivery outcomes, risks, and improvement opportunities to senior stakeholders.


About You


You bring strong people and practice leadership experience, including developing capability, setting clear expectations, and fostering a culture of collaboration, innovation, and accountability.
You are an experienced technology and delivery leader with a proven ability to influence technical outcomes across complex initiatives, balancing delivery priorities with long term sustainability, operability, and risk management.
You have a solid understanding of integration technologies and patterns across hybrid environments, including APIs, data and application integration, and middleware platforms, and are comfortable guiding and challenging solution design without being hands‑on.
You bring experience implementing, evolving, or scaling enterprise integration platforms in complex environments, including technologies such as IBM Sterling Integrator, MQ, MuleSoft, Azure Logic Apps, and Azure API Management.
You are experienced in governing and evolving delivery practices, standards, patterns, and automation approaches to improve quality, consistency, and efficiency across multiple initiatives.
You work effectively with technical professionals within your practice, as well as project managers, architecture, operations, and vendors, influencing decisions and resolving delivery and technical risks.
You bring a strong understanding of Financial Services, Government, or similarly regulated environments, including relevant policies, risk frameworks, and compliance obligations, and can guide teams to deliver compliant outcomes.
You understand the importance of operational readiness, and can ensure solutions are supportable, reliable, and fit for ongoing operation.
You bring a pragmatic understanding of portfolio and project delivery, applying waterfall and agile approaches to enable effective delivery rather than enforcing methodology.
You have strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with experience providing assurance and clear advice to senior leaders.


Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.


Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

Job Title: Senior Security Consultant - Data Security Focus
Ad Text:
Hybrid | Sydney | Exciting Data Security Role
 
Are you a seasoned cyber security professional who thrives on solving complex problems, influencing strategy, and delivering real-world outcomes?

Ad Text:
The Opportunity

Join a high‑performing Insurance Capability Team that plays a critical role in strengthening the performance, sustainability and integrity of the ReturntoWork Scheme in South Australia.

As the Insurance Capability, Strategic Advisor, you will lead strategic, scheme‑wide capability initiatives that enable Insurance leaders and teams - including our Claims Agents - to deliver improved outcomes for injured workers and employers. This role is central to identifying capability risks, designing practical and strategic solutions, and embedding systems that build a skilled, sustainable workforce across the Scheme.

Working closely with ReturntoWorkSA leaders, Claims Agents, internal business units and external stakeholders, you will turn strategic intent into operational reality. You will design and operationalise capability frameworks, lead complex projects, and provide strategic insights that support informed decision‑making across the Insurance function.

If you are energised by strategic problem‑solving, enjoy influencing at senior levels and want to make a meaningful impact on the performance of the Scheme, we encourage you to apply.

This role is offered as a two-year, full-time contract position.

The Position

As an Insurance Capability, Strategic Advisor, you will:

Optimise Insurance Capability

Design and maintain strategic systems that identify and analyse capability gaps across Insurance and the broader Scheme
Lead strategic initiatives that address capability risks and strengthen operational performance
Contribute to the design and review of key claims management systems and tools
Develop practical resources to support the application of legislative tests
Research best‑practice capability approaches across comparable schemes
Partner with People & Culture to identify talent needs and workforce risks

Enable Insurance Leadership Strategy

Work with Insurance leaders to evaluate scheme performance and identify emerging risks
Provide insights, tools, forums and data that support timely and informed strategic decisions
Support leaders to build strategic capability and deliver on Insurance strategic

Capability Data Management & Analysis

Establish and maintain robust capability and customer service data systems including Insurance Customer Experience
Produce monthly reporting and work with leaders to analyse trends and emerging risks
Develop and enable strategies to address identified risks

About You

You are a proactive and experienced strategic leader who is motivated by improving performance through people, systems and capability. You bring a balance of analytical thinking, operational understanding and strong stakeholder influence.

You will bring:

Expert knowledge of insurance scheme(s) and/or workers compensation (SA scheme experience desirable)
Experience designing and leading strategic programs or projects at a senior level
Strong research, analytical and problem‑solving capability
High‑level communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Ability to translate complex insights into practical, actionable solutions
Experience navigating organisational, legislative or technological change
Strong planning, organisation and governance capability
Tertiary qualifications in business, commerce, HR, law or a related field (preferred)

Further information

Candidates will also be assessed based on their ability to demonstrate personal and professional alignment with ReturnToWorkSA’s organisational values which are critical to our success: We care about people and sustainability; We are inclusive and innovative; We listen to understand; and We own our actions.

Remuneration

The total remuneration package for this Senior Officer position starts from $162,700.00 (inclusive of superannuation).

The final offer will be commensurate with the successful candidate’s qualifications, skills and experience.

 Insurance Capability, Strategic Advisor - Job Description

How to Apply

Please submit a cover letter demonstrating your key capabilities and experience, supported by your current resume, addressed to Jayne Gowland by 5:00pm, 29 April 2026.

For further information, please contact recruitment@rtwsa.com or phone Jayne on 0438 376 011.

Shortlisting and interviews will commence immediately, and the role may close early if a preferred candidate is identified.

About ReturnToWorkSA

At ReturnToWorkSA, our purpose is to empower and support South Australians impacted by work injury. Our services and those delivered by our claims agents are designed to provide early intervention support to workers and employers following a work injury to ensure the worker can recover and return to work as quickly as possible. We manage the collection of premium payments from registered employers and our invested funds to ensure we maintain a viable financial position that enables the delivery of quality support and services to injured workers.

In addition, we are the regulator of the Return to Work Scheme and play the key role of protecting the integrity and fairness of the Scheme by monitoring and enforcing compliance with the Act and providing education and support about the health benefits of work.

Why join us? 

Experience a workplace culture that rewards your contribution and provides the development and support to help you reach your potential. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where our people can genuinely make a difference to the lives of South Australians affected by work injury. At ReturnToWorkSA, you can expect a range of benefits including:

An extensive wellbeing program
access to a broad range of wellbeing seminars, webinars and information sessions
a free onsite gym and bike storage
annual vaccinations, skin checks, seasonal fruit and corporate cup challenge
discounted corporate private health insurance
and a proactive EAP provider
Flexible work options including part time and working from home
Access to learning and development programs to enhance your personal and professional skill set and career development
Modern offices with excellent amenities in the Adelaide CBD

Inclusion and acceptance

ReturnToWorkSA is an equal opportunity employer, valuing difference and embracing diversity and we promote flexible ways of working, applicants are encouraged to discuss any workplace adjustments that might be needed as well as the flexible working arrangements for this role.

Still not sure?

We are proud to be recognised as an Endorsed Employer for All Women by WORK180. Research indicates that up to 60% of women and underrepresented groups may hesitate to proceed, even after completing a draft application. We firmly believe that diversity enhances the strength of every team, so even if you don’t tick all the boxes we still want to hear from you.

Coronado Global Resources is a world-leading producer of high-quality metallurgical coal, operating in the Bowen Basin (Queensland, Australia) and the Central Appalachian region (USA). About the Role

Working closely with our operational and finance teams, you'll ensure the information you provide is accurate, practical, and easy to act on, helping the business understand its performance and make informed decisions every day.

What You Will Do

Manage transition from SAC Forecasting, Budgeting and Planning into the Corporate TM1 landscape

Collaborate with stakeholders across the organisation to understand reporting needs and turn them into clear, actionable analytics.
Identify opportunities to improve reporting processes and analytics practices, helping the business work more efficiently.
Support period-end, close and management reporting while maintaining compliance with corporate controls.

About You

Strong hands-on experience in TM1 development and administration
Solid background in finance systems and/or process improvement roles
Experience using Cubewise Pulse and Arc applications
Skilled in integration tools and data modelling
Exposure to ERP systems, ideally SAP S/4HANA
Familiarity with SAP Analytics Cloud highly regarded
Strong problem-solving ability with clear communication across stakeholders to define reporting and analytics needs
Confident delivering training and supporting users to adopt reporting and analytics tools across corporate and operational teams
Driven by continuous improvement, learning, and enabling smarter, data-led decision making

Why Join Us

Work with a globally recognised coal producer and be part of projects that impact operations.
Enjoy our six-star eco-certified Brisbane CBD office with premium facilities and river views.
Collaborate with a supportive, high-performing team in a dynamic and innovative environment.
Access professional development, flexibility, wellness programmes and corporate benefits.
Thrive in a culture rooted in collaboration, accountability, respect and excellence.

Apply today and take your career to the next level with Coronado.

Are you ready to shape the future of data architecture and help build a modern, scalable, and insights-driven enterprise?

We’re seeking an experienced Senior Data Architect to lead the design and delivery of next-generation data solutions — from data lakes and integration frameworks to advanced analytics platforms.

As the Senior Data Architect, you’ll play a key role in defining and delivering the organisation’s enterprise data architecture. In addition, you’ll ensure data is structured, governed, and leveraged effectively to support operational and strategic decision-making.

You’ll provide architectural leadership across integration, data warehousing, analytics, and governance while fostering collaboration between business units, technical teams, and senior leadership.

Key Responsibilities of the Senior Data Architect will include:
Develop and maintain enterprise-wide data architecture strategies, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with business goals.
Lead solution design for data platforms, integrations, data lakes, and BI/reporting systems using modern architectures (batch, streaming, APIs, microservices).
Create conceptual, logical, and physical data models across structured and unstructured data using best-practice frameworks (Inmon, Kimball, Data Vault).
Define and enforce data standards for quality, metadata, security, privacy, and compliance.
Architect data solutions across Azure, Snowflake, Informatica, and hybrid/multi-cloud environments.
Collaborate with stakeholders and technical teams to translate business needs into data-driven solutions.
Guide and mentor engineering teams on CI/CD, automation, and modern data engineering practices.
Optimise data systems for performance, scalability, and reliability.
Promote and embed a strong data culture across the organisation.
Skills & Experience required to measure success:
7+ years’ experience in data architecture, solution design, and enterprise data management.
Proven success architecting large-scale BI, analytics, and integration platforms.
Strong knowledge of cloud and hybrid ecosystems (Azure, AWS, Snowflake, Informatica, Databricks).
Expertise in data modelling (Inmon, Kimball, Data Vault 2.0).
Experience with data engineering tools, CI/CD, and DevOps practices.
Deep understanding of governance, metadata, and master data management.
Experience with modern integration approaches (APIs, event-driven, streaming).
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and leadership capability

Qualifications:
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field (required).
Postgraduate qualification in Data Science or Information Systems (highly regarded).
Certifications in Microsoft, Azure, Snowflake, or Informatica (desirable).
Apply now to shameem.prinsloo@troocoo.com if you’re passionate about leading enterprise-scale data architecture and building data ecosystems that enable real business impact!

Company description:



The Metcash story began from the humble origins of our first family-owned corner store in 1927. And for almost 100 years since then, we've been backing independents and strengthening the communities we operate in.

Today, Metcash (ASX: MTS) is Australia and New Zealand's leading independent retail partner and wholesaler in the food, liquor, hardware, and tools industries, with sales of $19.5 billion in FY25. We provide our network with marketing, purchasing, warehousing, distribution and systems support



Job description:



At Metcash, you're part of something bigger. We've got the heart of a small business with the strength and support of a big business.

When you join Metcash, your work makes a real difference to independent businesses and local communities, empowering you to:

Grow your career with tailored development programs
Thrive your way with flexible work options
Make your mark in a high-performing, values-led team

Australian Liquor Marketers (ALM) is the Liquor wholesale division of Metcash. We are the largest wide range wholesaler to the independent liquor trade. Being part of something bigger in the ALM team is all about making a huge difference for the local retailers, businesses and communities we service. And while you're championing successful independents, the wider Metcash family is behind you with a sea of opportunities to develop yourself, grow your career, and make your mark.

About the Opportunity

Reporting into the Senior FP & A Manager, the Senior FP&A Analyst delivers high impact financial insights and forward-looking analysis. The successful individual will drive commercial performance across the ALM pillar, while strengthening financial processes and reporting to support faster, better decision making. This is a full-time, permanent role based at the Macquarie Park office, with three days per week onsite.

In this Role

Identify EBIT/Gross Revenue ratio improvement opportunities, including cost savings and revenue mix optimisation.
In conjunction with Senior FP&A Manager partner and cross functional partners assist in modelling and developing longer term strategic objectives
Review YTD performance and provide YTG recommendations at state and customer level based on risks and opportunities.
Partner with State teams to update forecasts with future costs in line with latest performance.
Analyse and provide insights on sales variances to budget/forecast, highlighting customer under/overperformance and risks.
Analyse rebate variances, including product and supplier mix impacts.
Provide insights and recommendations for cost of sales over/underspend and identify ongoing risks or opportunities.
Consolidate state balance sheets, review variances, and provide visibility of key movements.
Prepare QBR decks and monthly performance insights.

You're Likely a Match If

Proven FP&A experience within an FMCG Company - Liquor Industry advantageous
Demonstrated experience of leading budgeting and forecasting including the ability to challenge and influence outcomes
Strong level of business partnering with Senior Management
Strong understanding of financial processes & P&L (Dr/Cr, BS, cashflow)
Power BI experience desirable

What's on offer

We're proud to offer a workplace that supports your well-being, growth, and belonging:

🏖️ Extra Leave: Enjoy a 5th week of annual leave, 2 well-being days, plus 1 volunteer day annually

📚 Career Growth: Endless learning and development opportunities

👶 Parental Leave: 12 weeks of gender-neutral paid leave for primary carers

🌈 Inclusive Culture: Bronze Employer for LGBTQ Inclusion

⚖️ Neutral Pay Gap: Gender pay gap under 5% (WGEA 2024-2025)

🧠 Mental Health: Gold accreditation by Mental Health Australia

🏡 Flexibility: Flex Ready and Family Friendly Workplace accredited

About Us

Join us and be part of something bigger!

Metcash is Australia's leading wholesale distribution company, with $19.5 billion in sales (FY25) and a proud spot in the ASX Top 125.

We believe in the power of independent, family-owned businesses to shape vibrant communities. Through our food, liquor, and hardware pillars, we help retailers become the ‘Best Store in Their Town' by providing merchandising, operational and marketing support.

We're committed to diversity, inclusion, and sustainability — and we welcome applicants from all backgrounds, including First Nations. If you need support during the application process, just let us know. Your privacy and dignity are always respected.

Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, delivering more than 11 million services to our customers every day across mobile, broadband and digital solutions.

The Compliance Program Reporting Lead plays a pivotal role in supporting Optus' Compliance Program by delivering high‑quality, executive‑ready reporting and insights across a complex regulatory environment. Reporting to the Director, Delivery Lead, this role acts as the central point for compliance program reporting, providing clear visibility of program progress, compliance status, risks and issues.

Responsible for owning and standardizing reporting frameworks, templates and artefacts, the role partners closely with project and business teams to embed consistent, transparent reporting practices. Working alongside Compliance, Legal and delivery teams, the position also supports the practical application of the Compliance Management Framework, monitors adherence to regulatory obligations and facilitates timely escalation and resolution of issues, balancing regulatory rigour with pragmatic, business‑aligned outcomes.

Your day to day will consist of

Own and maintain Compliance Program reporting standards, templates and artefacts, ensuring consistent, accurate and executive‑ready outputs

Collate, validate and analyse compliance and program data to produce regular and ad‑hoc reports for senior leadership, governance forums and regulators

Partner closely with project managers and business stakeholders to support timely, high‑quality reporting and embed strong reporting disciplines

Monitor project alignment with the Compliance Management Framework, identifying risks, issues and early warning indicators, and escalating as required

Drive continuous improvement in reporting processes, data quality and insights, working with Compliance, Legal and PMO teams to support effective program delivery

What makes you perfect for the role?

Extensive experience in compliance program reporting, delivering clear, executive‑ready insights across complex, highly regulated environments

Strong expertise in establishing and improving reporting frameworks, standards and governance artefacts to support senior leadership and regulatory requirements

Proven ability to partner with project managers, Compliance, Legal and PMO teams to embed consistent reporting disciplines and compliance practices

Highly analytical, with a track record of identifying risks, trends and early warning indicators through data‑driven insights

Confident stakeholder influencer, able to balance regulatory rigor with pragmatic, commercially aligned advice across all levels of the organization

Competitive remuneration and employee discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and unique shopping discounts with our retail partners.

Vibrant and collaborative office campus that includes cafes, convenience store, chill out zones, GP, post office, gym and on-site childcare centre.

Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.

Build meaningful connections through employee-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).

We are growing fast and looking for exceptional talent to help shape the future of AI.

The Opportunity

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will be a key technical leader within our advanced analytics and AI group. You will design, build, and deploy sophisticated machine learning systems—particularly LLM‑driven solutions—that solve high‑value, real‑world problems for enterprise clients.

You’ll be part of a culture that prizes technical excellence, creativity, and continuous learning. Beyond technical depth, you will influence strategy, mentor emerging talent, and help shape our approach to responsible and scalable AI.

Your work will directly contribute to AI systems that have the potential to transform industries—and society.

What You’ll Do

Architect, build, and deploy machine learning models and LLM systems that drive meaningful impact for our products and users

Analyse complex, large‑scale structured and unstructured datasets to uncover insights and opportunities

Enhance LLM output quality across diverse use cases, including optimisation of multi‑step reasoning chains

Lead MLOps initiatives including model performance tracking, benchmark design, quality assurance, and compliance

Influence long‑term data and AI strategy, including governance, ethical AI, and scalable frameworks

Collaborate closely with engineering, product, and executive stakeholders to deliver innovative, production‑ready solutions

Mentor and uplift junior data scientists, setting standards for excellence

About You

You are a seasoned Data Scientist (5+ years) with deep hands‑on experience and a track record of delivering impactful ML and AI outcomes. You thrive in ambiguity, communicate exceptionally well, and enjoy partnering with cross‑functional teams to deliver measurable results.

You balance scientific rigour with practical execution, and you bring a curiosity that keeps you constantly exploring new research—especially in the fast‑moving LLM space.

You’ll be successful if you have:

A Master’s or Doctorate in Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science or a related discipline

Experience from within AI sector, ideally agentic AI

Strong experience with NLP techniques: LSTMs, CNNs, transformers, topic modelling, supervised NLP

Commercial experience with modern LLM ecosystems (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, RAG pipelines, multi‑agent frameworks, GPT‑based systems)

Python experience focused on machine learning and NLP. Knowledge of other software languages is highly beneficial

Ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear, actionable insights for non‑technical stakeholders

Empathy, humility, and a growth mindset—open to feedback and committed to collaboration

Comfort working in an agile, fast‑evolving environment

Ability to attend our Sydney CBD office 1–2 days per week

Why Join Us?

Shape the future of AI alongside world‑class technical and commercial leaders

Work with cutting‑edge models, frameworks, and R&D pipelines

Contribute to solutions used by global enterprises

Grow rapidly in a company with significant backing and momentum

Join a high‑trust, high‑performance culture built around craft, ownership, and impact

If you’re driven by innovation, inspired by big challenges, and ready to build AI systems that matter—we’d love to meet you.

For a quicker response, we encourage you to apply directly through our website at https://www.futuresecure.ai/. There, you can watch a video about AI workers and explore the career opportunities we have available.

Job Title: Senior Vice President of Retail Products
Ad Text:
About Us

Join oneZero, a global leader in multi-asset enterprise trading technology, trusted by over 200 financial institutions worldwide. With Operations actoss USA, UK, Singapore and Australia, we empower banks, brokerages and hedge funds with a highly customisable, ultra-low-latency platform that sits at the core of modern financial markets.

At oneZero, we don’t just build cutting-edge technology, we create an environment where exceptional people can thrive. Our culture is collaborative, high-performing and driven by real impact. It’s a place where your ideas are valued, your expertise is recognised, and your work genuinely makes a difference.

We’re proud to be consistently recognised as a top employer:

Best Places to Work — Business Intelligence Group (3 consecutive years)

2025 Great Place To Work® Australia Certified™

What You’ll Enjoy

We believe people do their best work when they feel supported, rewarded and inspired:

Competitive bonus structure

Flexible hybrid working arrangements

Company-provided parking

Daily lunch in the office

Private health insurance

Monthly wellbeing program subscription

Key Responsibilities 

Define and execute product strategy for oneZero’s Retail segment, aligned to company growth objectives. 

Continuously assess evolving business models, market structure, and competitive dynamics across the value streams relevant to our target customers. 

Translate customer insights into clear prioritization, roadmaps, and new product capabilities that deliver high value. 

Champion methods and priorities that ensure high quality in our products. 

Drive and formalize pricing policy and process with an emphasis on maintaining system integrity. 

Maintain and evolve pricing and packaging in collaboration with senior management to support product and market evolution. 

Identify, evaluate, and incubate new product concepts and adjacent opportunities. 

Contribute to make/buy/partner decisions and manage relevant strategic partnerships. 

Lead and develop a team of product managers, instilling strong product discipline, strategic thinking, and customer intimacy. 

Provide cross-functional leadership across Engineering, Sales, and Support to ensure successful delivery and lifecycle management. 

Own commercial performance for the retail segment in partnership with executive leadership. 

Measure the value our existing products and new capabilities add for customers and use the data to fuel further innovation and continuous improvement. 

Required Experience and Capabilities 

10+ years serving retail brokerage firms (multi-asset including FX, metals, crypto, CFDS, and beyond) in a product, commercial, or operating role. 

Strong preference for experience in a product role at a technology vendor serving this market; outstanding candidates from brokerages will also be considered. 

Proven product management skillset, including strategy development and roadmap ownership. 

Demonstrated ability to listen deeply, diagnose root business problems, and prioritize effectively — including confidently declining low-impact requests. 

Strong commercial orientation, with experience defining and influencing pricing, positioning, and revenue outcomes. 

Experience leading and developing high-performing teams. 

Technical fluency in trading infrastructure (connectivity, execution workflows, risk management, APIs, data pipelines) is a strong plus. 

Comfort and skill in speaking at industry events or interviews 

Bachelor’s Degree in technical, hard science, or business fields.  Advanced degrees are a plus. 

So, what are you waiting for? APPLY NOW to chat to our team!

Brennan. Where true performance thrives. 
At Brennan, we believe that how technology is delivered is every bit as important as what the technology is. We focus on creating real and relevant value for customers with solutions that fit their specific needs and always reflect their true interests. 
It’s a claim backed by our True Performance System – a way of working engineered to get us closer, and deliver better, for our customers and their actual experience of technology. 

Why join Brennan 
True performance for our customers starts with a true belief in our people. 
It’s why we’ve structured our business to help our teams, and their talents, shine bright. It's why we’ve created a workplace where people of all backgrounds, beliefs and experiences are welcomed and empowered. And it’s why we’ve built an organisation where real innovation makes a genuine impact and generates true rewards for our team members. 

True rewards 
In addition to competitive remuneration, Brennan offers extensive benefits, including: 

Culture Awards that recognise excellence.
Brennan Daredevils – our annual, all-expenses paid trip awarded to our top performers and outstanding contributors.
Vibrant, fun social activities.
An environment that embraces learning and development. 
The role
You will provide expert advice and guidance to clients, helping them achieve their business, IT and customer goals.

Your role will involve working closely with clients to identify their needs and developing strategies to address those needs.

This may include analysing data, creating new processes and procedures, aligning business requirements with technical delivery, and identifying opportunities for improvement.

What You’ll Be Doing

Engage with client stakeholders to plan and facilitate workshops, effectively eliciting requirements and translating insights into actionable recommendations for improvement.
Independently lead and deliver client engagements, effectively managing timelines, budgets and forecasted effort.
Collaborate closely with clients and internal teams to understand business challenges and design practical tailored solutions.
Conduct structured analysis across digital transformation, AI adoption, automation strategies and broader technology uplift initiatives.
Design digital, IT and AI strategies aligned to organisational priorities, risk profiles and long-term business objectives.
Develop robust business cases, transformation roadmaps and detailed implementation plans to support strategic initiatives.


What We’re Looking For

Minimum 5 years of experience in leading business advisory and management consulting engagements, or similar role.
Strong experience in successfully leading consulting engagements that have produced positive and tangible business outcomes.
Ability to develop, lead and facilitate stakeholder workshops to identify problems and develop solutions using contemporary approaches.
A strong client focus and a strength in taking ownership to proactively monitor customer satisfaction while negotiating mutually beneficial outcomes.
High level of organisation and time management to enable you to prioritise effectively and to successfully deliver multiple client engagements concurrently.
Extensive experience of the broader technology ecosystem, common approaches to managing technology, modern technology concepts and emerging technologies.
Don’t worry if you don’t tick every box, we’d still love to hear from you!

Note: As part of our hiring process, you will be required to undertake a National Criminal History Check. 

Brennan is an equal-opportunity employer.

We’ve been proudly serving Australian communities since 1914 and have grown to become a top 30-listed company on the ASX with a portfolio of iconic homegrown brands. At the heart of our continued success is over 115,000 team members working across the country, all contributing to bringing our customers smiles. You’ll not only get to see your work make a difference, you’ll be part of a team working together to help Australians eat and live better every day.

About the team

Our Finance team are a vital partner in every aspect of our business, providing commercial insights to drive our success and influence everything we do. With presence in every department, we analyse performance, and deliver actionable insights that shape our strategic decisions.

About the role


As the Finance Business Partner - Sustainability, you will be providing trusted financial partnership to support Coles’ ESG and sustainability targets, embedding sustainability within our commercial teams. Your key responsibilities will include:

Partner with Commercial Sustainability as a trusted Finance Business Partner, providing financial information, analysis, tools and insights to support informed decision-making and business strategy.
Challenge thinking and influence outcomes through commercial insight, shaping and driving key strategic initiatives.
Ensure ESG objectives are embedded across planning, capital allocation, risk management and decision-making, supporting delivery of Coles’ externally committed sustainability targets.

Support end-to-end project delivery including funding approvals, forecasting, performance tracking and risk identification.
Deliver monthly and quarterly financial reporting for senior forums including LT sessions, Commercial Leadership Team packs and Quarterly Business Reviews.
Build strong stakeholder alignment across Finance and Commercial teams, engaging with GMs, Heads of, Senior Finance Managers, FBPs, project managers and cross-functional partners across risk, strategy and financial control.

About you and your skills



You are a commercially savvy finance professional, with a strong outcome mindset, capable of simplifying complex sustainability and financial information for senior stakeholders across large, complex organisations. The ideal candidate will have:



CA, CPA or equivalent professional accounting qualification.
Experience in a business partnering role, ideally within retail, FMCG or environments with enterprise‑wide sustainability commitments.
Familiarity with climate change economics and decarbonisation levers desirable.
Demonstrated experience with sustainability decisions impacting margin and pricing.

Proven ability to analyse data and translate it into meaningful, actionable commercial insights.

Background in implementing change and continuous improvement initiatives that deliver value and improved business outcomes.

We also offer additional periods of double discount (10%) at various times throughout the year. As well as hotdeals exclusive to team members that translate into additional savings.
Reward through recognition: Give and receive recognition, linked to our Coles values, through our digital recognition platform ‘mythanks’. You can accumulate points to redeem in the online shop for exciting gifts and electronic gift cards from an extensive range of retailers.
Opportunities for learning and development: No matter where you start within our diverse business, you’ll have experiences, exposure, and education to satisfy you. Discover and explore a variety of career development programs and job-specific training.
Paid parental leave: We understand how important your life outside work is and offer permanent team members paid parental leave to support you in balancing work and family.
Investment in your future: Our annual team member share plan offer allows eligible team members to make regular pre-tax salary sacrifice deductions to purchase Coles Group shares.

Job Title: Data Modeller - Payroll Compliance and Remediation
Ad Text:
PM-Partners group is a specialist Project Management Consultancy. We have a unique way of running projects and providing training and advice, so our clients get the best results with less cost and risk. Each year we deliver over $3B of projects, train over 10,000 people, and help some of Australia’s leading brands improve their PMOs.




Engagement: 6 month fixed term contract 

Employment Type: Full Time 38 hours

Work Location: Remote or Sydney

Start Date: ASAP




About the Role

As a Data Modeller, you will support payroll remediation initiatives by designing and delivering high-quality data models that enable accurate underpayment analysis and back-pay calculations.

You will develop payroll remediation models using Snowflake SQL, aligned to industrial relations obligations and legislative requirements, to identify compliance gaps and calculate potential underpayments. Leveraging your expertise in Australian payroll, you will translate Awards, Enterprise Agreements, and IR pay rules into structured, auditable data assets that support transparent calculations, reconciliation, and regulatory compliance.




Ideal Candidates

Ideally looking for someone who has work in a software delivery space with:

3-5+ years’ experience in data modelling, data engineering, or analytics roles, with a strong focus on complex, time-based data
Demonstrated experience on Australian payroll remediation, payroll transformation, or underpayment programs, with hands-on involvement in historical recalculations.
Strong Australian payroll domain knowledge, including:
Modern awards and/or Enterprise Agreements
Penalties, overtime, allowances, loadings
Averaging arrangements and minimum engagement rules
Superannuation (OTE vs non-OTE) and basic tax/charges concepts E.g Payroll tax, SGC, PAYG
Proven ability to design logical and physical data models that support award and pay rule calculations.
Advanced SQL skills, with experience preparing calculation-ready datasets for rule engines or large-scale recalculation logic.
Experience integrating and reconciling data from payroll systems and time-and-attendance platforms.
Strong understanding of effective-dated data, historical rate changes, and lookback period recalculations
Experience producing audit-ready documentation, including data dictionaries, mappings, Decisions and assumptions.

Core Responsibilities

Design and maintain calculation-ready payroll data models to support large-scale payroll remediation activities.
Structure and transform payroll, time and attendance, and HR data into a canonical remediation model suitable for historical recalculations.
Prepare data assets that support:
Award and Enterprise Agreement pay rules
Penalty, overtime, and allowance calculations
Apportionment of hours and pay across time boundaries
Averaging arrangements and higher-of comparisons
Recalculation across multi-year remediation lookback periods
Analyse and map data from multiple payroll and timekeeping systems, documenting source-to-target mappings and transformation logic.
Ensure payroll data is effective-dated, auditable, and traceable for quality assurance requirements.
Implement data quality checks and support reconciliation of recalculated outcomes against original payroll payments or compliance models.
Produce clear documentation including data dictionaries, model diagrams, and assumptions to support audit, legal, and Fair Work scrutiny.
Work collaboratively with IR, Legal, Payroll, and remediation calculation teams to validate data outputs and resolve data issues.
Deliver agreed modelling and data outputs within scope, timelines, and governance frameworks typical of payroll remediation programs.

If you feel you meet our selection criteria, please submit your resume via SEEK application

ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking an experienced and commercially astute Pricing Leader to join Ampcontrol. This role can be based out of North Sydney or Tomago.

This is a pivotal, enterprise‑wide role responsible for transforming how Ampcontrol prices its Engineer‑to‑Order (ETO) projects, products and services. You will lead the shift from traditional cost‑plus pricing to a more strategic, market‑ and value‑based pricing approach, embedding pricing as a core commercial capability across the organisation.

Partnering closely with Sales, Engineering, Finance and Operations, you will provide strategic pricing leadership, support complex bids and tenders, improve margin performance, and strengthen Ampcontrol’s competitiveness as we continue to grow organically and through M&A.

If you are passionate about value creation, commercial discipline and driving meaningful change in complex environments, we’d love to hear from you.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Lead the transformation from cost‑plus to market‑ and value‑based pricing across ETO projects, products and services.
Develop and implement pricing strategies, frameworks and governance that improve margin performance and commercial discipline.
Provide strategic pricing leadership and commercial support for complex bids, tenders and high‑value opportunities.
Design scalable pricing models for ETO, standard, configurable and services offerings, incorporating risk and value insights.
Analyse customer, market and competitor pricing trends to inform go‑to‑market and deal decisions.
Build pricing capability across the business by coaching, enabling and partnering with Sales, Engineering, Finance and Operations.

ABOUT YOU

Education & Qualifications

Tertiary qualification in Finance, Economics, Engineering, Business, Commerce or a related discipline.

Technical Skills

Proven pricing leadership experience in industrial, engineering, manufacturing or project‑based environments.
Strong experience in ETO or complex project pricing.
Demonstrated capability in developing services pricing models.
Advanced financial and commercial modelling skills, including margin, risk and scenario analysis.
Experience supporting bids, tenders and commercial negotiations.
Ability to design and implement pricing governance frameworks, tools and processes.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate insights into practical, commercial decisions.

Experience & Attributes

Proven success transitioning organisations from cost‑plus to market or value‑based pricing.
Strong communication and stakeholder influencing skills at a senior leadership level.
Ability to lead change and embed new ways of working in complex, evolving organisations.
Commercially minded, outcome‑driven and confident operating in ambiguous environments.
Collaborative, proactive and highly credible with cross‑functional teams.

 EMPLOYEE BENEFITS

At Ampcontrol, we believe great work deserves great rewards. When you join us, you’ll have access to a wide range of benefits designed to support your wellbeing, lifestyle, and career growth, including:

Flexible work arrangements to suit your lifestyle
Paid parental leave to support your family
Salary packaging options to help you make the most of your income
Purchased leave for greater work-life balance
Discounted private health care
Wellbeing programs and employee discounts
Education assistance and career development support

READY TO CHALLENGE THE FUTURE?

Then apply now! If you are intrigued or excited by what you have read, we want to hear from you.

Learn more about what it’s like to work at Ampcontrol by visiting www.ampcontrolgroup.com
